about

CineCRCE 2026 is a national-level 75-hour short film competition that challenges students across India to push the limits of their creativity and craft.

Within a strictly defined 75-hour window, participants must conceptualise, script, shoot, edit, and submit a complete short film from scratch.

prize pool

60k+

INR  ·  Registration: INR 450 per team

01 — First

INR 25,000

02 — Second

INR 12,500

03 — Third

INR 7,500

Domain Awards

Best Acting Performance
Best Direction
Best Editing
Best Cinematography
Best Film of CRCE

registration

Open to all students. Form your team and begin. Only students are eligible.

  • 01Registration fee: INR 450 per team
  • 02Minimum team size: 1 member
  • 03Maximum team size: 10 members
  • 04One team per participant only
  • 05Student enrolment required

event format

fri 10 apr 2026
8:59 pm

Theme Reveal Competition begins immediately. Creative window opens.

10 – 13 apr 2026
75 hours

Filmmaking Window Script, shoot, edit, post-produce. No pre-made material.

sun 13 apr 2026
11:59 pm

Submission Deadline YouTube Unlisted + Google Form link. Late window: +4 hrs.

thu 16 apr 2026

Screening & Results Scores revealed transparently at the official screening.

submission requirements

  • Duration4 – 7 minutes including credits. Films outside this window incur point deductions.
  • ResolutionMinimum 1920 × 1080 (Full HD), horizontal only
  • UploadYouTube as Unlisted. Submit link via official Google Form
  • NamingTEAMNAME_FILMTITLE
  • Intro + CreditsCombined must not exceed 30 seconds
  • Late PolicyAccepted up to 4 hrs post-deadline. Penalty: 2 pts per 10 min, capped at 48 pts. After 4 hrs: not accepted.
  • DisclaimerMandatory at the start of every film. Films with intense themes, mild violence, or substance refs need an explicit disclaimer.
  • OriginalityAll content must be created within the 75-hour window. Plagiarism = immediate disqualification.

content guidelines

Strictly Prohibited

  • × Plagiarism or pre-existing footage
  • × AI-generated clips, scenes, or scripts
  • × Stock footage
  • × Hate speech, communal provocation, cuss words
  • × Targeting religion, caste, gender, race, or community
  • × Explicit sexual content or nudity
  • × Graphic gore or extreme violence
  • × Glorification of drugs or substance abuse

Contextually Permitted

  • ✓ Mild violence integral to the narrative
  • ✓ Sensitive dialogue used responsibly
  • ✓ Social commentary on difficult themes
  • ✓ Mild romantic interactions, handled respectfully
  • ✓ Extreme sequences depicted artistically, not visually

judging criteria

  • 01

    Storytelling & Narrative Clarity of plot, structure, pacing, audience connection

    20 pts
  • 02

    Theme Interpretation Effectiveness and depth in exploring the assigned theme

    20 pts
  • 03

    Screenplay & Scene Structure Writing quality, dialogue, scene construction, narrative flow

    20 pts
  • 04

    Direction Creative vision, execution, overall coherence

    20 pts
  • 05

    Cinematography Framing, lighting, camera work, visual aesthetics

    20 pts
  • 06

    Editing & Pacing Rhythm, transitions, continuity, narrative smoothness

    20 pts
  • 07

    Acting & Performance Authenticity, emotional depth, character portrayal

    20 pts
  • 08

    Sound Design Background score, sound effects, clarity, audio integration

    20 pts
  • 09

    Creativity & Originality Innovation, uniqueness of concept, fresh storytelling

    20 pts
  • 10

    Overall Impact Emotional resonance, memorability, lasting impression

    20 pts

Total score

200 pts

right to screen & authority

Participants grant the Organizers non-exclusive rights to screen submitted films for non-commercial purposes during the event and for official institutional use, including promotional screenings.

All decisions by the Organizing Committee regarding eligibility, content suitability, scoring, or disqualification are final and binding. No appeals or disputes shall be entertained.
